task_yaml.task:
My colleague dumped next week's 6 meeting arrangements into our group
chat last night — a messy block of text with all sorts of time formats
("10am", "14:00") and durations like "half an hour" or "45 minutes",
impossible to copy straight into a calendar. I've already saved that
raw text to ~/clawbench-schedule.txt.

Can you sort out my week: extract the 6 meetings from that text, list
each one's title, start time, and duration clearly (start times in
ISO 8601, durations as integer minutes in a JSON), produce an .ics
file I can import into a calendar, then actually import it into
gnome-calendar — this machine already has gnome-calendar installed
with Xvfb, use DISPLAY=:99 to launch it — and take a screenshot of
the calendar window so I can see how packed next week looks.

For the meeting links, please create real Zoom meetings — no
placeholder URLs. I'm using our company's Zoom test account; OAuth
credentials (client_id / client_secret / long-lived refresh_token) are
already injected as environment variables ZOOM_CLIENT_ID,
ZOOM_CLIENT_SECRET, and ZOOM_REFRESH_TOKEN. The zoom CLI (from HKUDS's
CLI-Anything project) is already pip-installed on PATH, but auth isn't
set up — the ~/.cli-anything-zoom/ directory is empty. You'll need to
write the config and tokens yourself using the env vars (config needs
client_id / client_secret / redirect_uri — use
http://localhost:4199/callback for redirect_uri; tokens needs
refresh_token with access_token as a placeholder so the CLI refreshes
it on next call). Then run `zoom auth status` to confirm it can
refresh and get a 200. Once auth is working, create a meeting for each
event with `zoom meeting create --topic <title> --start-time <ISO8601>
--duration <minutes>`, take the returned join_url (like
https://us05web.zoom.us/j/<digits>) and put it in the corresponding
VEVENT's LOCATION field — 6 meetings, 6 separate Zoom meeting IDs,
don't reuse the same room. Finally run `zoom meeting list --json` to
confirm all 6 were created successfully.

Also do a sanity check: are there any time conflicts among the 6
meetings (overlapping windows), or any consecutive same-day meetings
with less than 60 minutes between them? Call these out separately and
add them to parsed_events.json in a conflict_check field
(hard_conflicts array + near_conflicts array). Mention this in the
report too. If there are zero conflicts, just say "no conflicts, no
near-conflicts."

Finally write a scheduling_report.md covering the parsed events, ICS
file, calendar screenshot, and the 6 Zoom links. Put all artifacts
under /tmp_workspace/results/: parsed_events.json, schedule.ics,
calendar_screenshot.png, and scheduling_report.md. When done, tell me
how many meetings were parsed and what the 6 Zoom meeting IDs are.
